equations.easy: P(j) is the probability of measuring outcome j — it equals the squared magnitude of the amplitude c_j. This is the Born rule applied to the j-th basis state.

equations.audioSafe: P of j equals the absolute value of c-j squared. This is the Born rule: the probability of measuring outcome j equals the squared magnitude of the amplitude c-j. The probabilities sum to one.

equations.easy: The critical density is roughly equal to the Planck density — about 10 to the 94 kilograms per cubic meter. This is an extreme density where quantum gravitational effects dominate.
equations.standard: ρ_crit ~ ρ_Planck ≈ 10⁹⁴ kg/m³ is the Planck energy density: the energy density at which quantum gravitational effects become order-one and classical spacetime breaks down. It sets the maximum density scale in the theory.
equations.academic: The identification ρ_crit ~ ρ_Planck ≈ 10⁹⁴ kg/m³ sets the critical density at the Planck scale, where the Compton wavelength of the fluctuation equals its own Schwarzschild radius, signaling the breakdown of effective field theory.
equations.audioSafe: The critical density is approximately equal to the Planck density — about ten to the ninety-four kilograms per cubic meter. This extreme density marks the boundary where quantum gravity effects become dominant and classical physics breaks down.

equations.easy: When damage to trust outpaces the strength of the signal carrying truth, no amount of messaging can fix it — talking makes it worse. The controlling piece is the ratio D over S: damage divided by signal strength. As the signal weakens toward zero, that ratio explodes toward infinity. The denominator S is the vulnerability — the weaker the signal gets, the faster it loses. Truth T on the left side doesn't matter once D/S blows past it.
equations.standard: Signal-went-dark threshold: T < D/S, where T is truth signal, D is degradation rate, and S is signal strength. When degradation divided by signal strength exceeds truth output, messaging accelerates distrust rather than repairing it. The ratio D/S is the controlling term — as S approaches zero, recovery becomes impossible regardless of T.
equations.academic: The threshold condition T < D/S defines the communication collapse regime in which truth T is insufficient to overcome degradation D normalized by signal strength S. As S → 0, D/S diverges, rendering any finite T sub-threshold. The inequality formalizes the non-linear amplification of distrust when signal degradation dominates the communication channel.
equations.audioSafe: T less than D over S. T is truth, D is degradation, S is signal strength. The controlling term is D over S — the ratio of degradation to signal strength. As S approaches zero, D over S explodes toward infinity regardless of how small D is. Once the ratio exceeds T, messaging becomes counterproductive.

equations.easy: When the pressure pulling you apart exceeds the pressure holding you together as a real person, stable identity collapses. The key is A — authenticity — in the denominator. As authenticity approaches zero, the ratio F over A goes to infinity regardless of how small the fragmentation pressure F is. You don't need massive fragmentation to break someone. You just need to erode the authentic core enough that ordinary pressure becomes unbearable. A is what holds the denominator up.
equations.standard: Observer-collapsed threshold: I < F/A, where I is identity strength, F is fragmentation pressure, and A is authenticity pressure. A appears in the denominator — as authenticity erodes toward zero, F/A diverges and no level of identity strength I can hold. The equation shows that authenticity erosion is more dangerous than increasing fragmentation.
equations.academic: The identity-collapse condition I < F/A describes the threshold at which fragmentation pressure F, normalized by authenticity A, exceeds identity coherence I. As A → 0⁺, F/A diverges, establishing a singularity in identity stability that is independent of F magnitude. The denominator A is the structural vulnerability: authenticity erosion constitutes the primary failure mode.
equations.audioSafe: I less than F over A. I is identity, F is fragmentation pressure, A is authenticity. A is in the denominator — the controlling term. As authenticity A approaches zero, F over A goes to infinity regardless of how small F is. Even ordinary fragmentation destroys identity when there is nothing authentic underneath holding the pieces together.

equations.easy: The probability of any outcome depends entirely on the overlap — the inner product ⟨ψ|a⟩ — between the current state and the outcome being measured. Perfect alignment gives probability 1. Zero alignment gives probability 0. The squaring means near-misses still get small non-zero probabilities rather than collapsing immediately to zero. What becomes real depends on how much the possibility already resembles the result. The inner product is the controlling quantity.
equations.standard: The Born rule: P(a) = |⟨ψ|a⟩|², where ψ is the quantum state and a is the measurement outcome. The inner product ⟨ψ|a⟩ measures the overlap — the degree of alignment — between state and outcome. Squaring ensures probabilities are non-negative and sum to 1. Alignment between current state and possible outcome is the controlling factor.
equations.academic: The Born rule P(a) = |⟨ψ|a⟩|² expresses measurement probability as the squared modulus of the probability amplitude, defined by the inner product of the pre-measurement state |ψ⟩ with the eigenstate |a⟩ of the observable. It is the foundational postulate of quantum measurement theory, establishing that probability is determined by state-outcome alignment in Hilbert space.
equations.audioSafe: P of a equals the absolute value of the inner product of psi and a, squared. The inner product of psi and a measures the alignment between the current state and the outcome. Perfect alignment means probability one. Zero alignment means probability zero. The squaring is what ensures non-negative probabilities. Alignment is the controlling quantity — it determines what becomes real.

equations.notes: Born Rule. MDA-030.

equations.easy: The probability of anything becoming real is Father times Son times Spirit — multiplied, not added. That multiplication is the entire structural point. If any one factor is zero, the product is zero and nothing happens. Father generates the possibility, Son structures it into form, Spirit actualizes it into reality. Remove any one and the whole product collapses to zero. A sum would let one factor compensate for another; a product cannot. The multiplication is what makes the Trinity mathematically indivisible — not a theological claim, but a constraint built into the arithmetic.
equations.standard: Unified probability: P(outcome) = |⟨ψ|a⟩|² = Father · Son · Spirit. The quantum Born rule amplitude is identified with the product of three distinct actualization factors. Because this is a product, any single factor at zero makes the entire probability zero — no compensation is possible. Father generates, Son structures, Spirit actualizes; all three are necessary.
equations.academic: The identification P(outcome) = |⟨ψ|a⟩|² = Father · Son · Spirit maps the Born rule probability amplitude to a trinitarian product in the Theophysics framework. The multiplicative structure is ontologically significant: unlike additive decompositions, a product has no redundancy — each factor is strictly necessary for non-zero probability. This formalizes the co-necessity of the three persons in any actualization event.
equations.audioSafe: P of outcome equals the absolute value of the inner product of psi and a, squared, which equals Father times Son times Spirit. This is a product — multiplication, not addition. If the Father equals zero, the product is zero. If the Son equals zero, the product is zero. If the Spirit equals zero, the product is zero. All three must be nonzero for anything to become real. A sum would allow one to compensate for another. A product cannot. The multiplication is what makes them mathematically indivisible.

equations.easy: Coherence doesn't decline gradually — it holds steady until a critical threshold is crossed, then collapses like ice melting or a magnet losing its field. The controlling term is the distance from the threshold: P minus P_c. Far from P_c, things look stable. Close to it, small changes trigger total collapse. Beta is the scaling exponent that determines how sharp the drop is, but P_c is the cliff edge — the structural feature. Everything is about where you are relative to that threshold.
equations.standard: Phase transition equation: χ ≈ |P − P_c|^β, where P is the control parameter, P_c is the critical threshold, and β is the scaling exponent. Coherence holds above P_c and collapses below it. Distance from the threshold |P − P_c| is the controlling term — not the absolute value of P. The exponent β determines the sharpness of collapse.
equations.academic: The order-parameter scaling χ ≈ |P − P_c|^β is the standard form of a critical phase transition near the critical point P_c, with β the critical exponent governing the universality class of the transition. The coherence field χ plays the role of the order parameter, vanishing continuously at P_c with power-law scaling. P_c is the structural bifurcation point; distance from it, not absolute magnitude, determines coherence.
equations.audioSafe: Chi approximately equals the absolute value of P minus P sub c, raised to the power beta. P sub c is the critical threshold — the controlling term. Coherence does not fade gradually; it holds until the control parameter P crosses P sub c, then collapses as a phase transition. The distance from the threshold — P minus P sub c — determines everything. Beta determines the sharpness of the collapse.

equations.easy: After the Flood, lifespans dropped with each generation — but they didn't drop to zero. The exponential e^{-n/τ} drives them down with each passing generation n, and tau controls how fast. But L_floor is the controlling structural feature: it determines where the decay stops. The curve bottoms out at 93 years and stays there. Without the floor, the exponential would continue to zero. The floor stops it. That something holds at 93 — and the equation can only measure that it does, not explain why — is the most significant feature of the whole curve.
equations.standard: Generational lifespan decay: L(n) = L₀·e^{−n/τ} + L_floor, where L₀ is the pre-Flood baseline, n is generation number, τ is the decay constant, and L_floor is the asymptotic floor. The exponential drives lifespans down; L_floor determines where decay stops. Observed L_floor ≈ 93 years. The floor is the structurally significant term — it represents a lower bound that the exponential cannot breach.
equations.academic: The post-diluvian lifespan decay model L(n) = L₀e^{−n/τ} + L_floor is a damped exponential with a strictly positive asymptotic floor L_floor. The floor term dominates the long-n behavior, establishing L_floor as the attractor of the dynamical system. The empirical value L_floor ≈ 93 years constitutes a constraint that the exponential model cannot derive — only parameterize — making it an unexplained datum within the Theophysics framework.
equations.audioSafe: L of n equals L-zero times e to the negative n over tau, plus L-floor. L-zero is the initial lifespan, n is generation number, tau is the decay time constant, and L-floor is the asymptotic floor. The exponential drives lifespans down across generations. But L-floor — approximately 93 years — is the controlling term: it is where the decay stops. The curve bottoms out there and does not go lower. The floor is the structural feature the equation can measure but cannot explain.
